text: We cannot agree to these terms unless you include a {{c1::termination clause}} for convenience.
explanation: A termination clause is a contract provision that defines the conditions under which the agreement can be ended. For convenience means it can be terminated without needing a specific cause or breach.
usage:
Pattern: termination clause / terminate for convenience
  • The contract contains a 30-day termination clause.
  • Either party may terminate the agreement for convenience with written notice.
spanish: No podemos aceptar estos términos a menos que incluya una cláusula de rescisión por conveniencia.

text: During the review, he negotiated: "Based on the {{c1::market rate}} and my contributions, I'd like to adjust my {{c2::compensation package}} to include remote-work fringe benefits."
explanation: Market rate is the standard salary for a role. **Compensation package** includes base salary, bonuses, and benefits. **Fringe benefits** are extra benefits (like gym memberships or health insurance).
usage:
Pattern: negotiate a compensation package based on market rate / include fringe benefits
  • Our company offers an attractive compensation package with great fringe benefits.
  • The offer is below the current market rate for senior developers.
spanish: Durante la revisión, negoció: "Basándome en la tarifa del mercado y mis contribuciones, me gustaría ajustar mi paquete de compensación para incluir beneficios adicionales de trabajo remoto".

text: We are willing to make concessions, but we need you to match us on the {{c1::payment terms}}.
explanation: 'Payment terms' are the conditions under which a seller will complete a sale, specifying when payment is due and any discounts or fees (e.g. Net 30).
usage:
Pattern: payment terms / Net 30 / Net 60
  • The contract specifies payment terms of Net 45.
  • We negotiated better payment terms with our main vendor.
spanish: Estamos dispuestos a hacer concesiones, pero necesitamos que esté de acuerdo con nosotros en las condiciones de pago.

text: Our goal is to reach a win-win agreement that benefits both parties in the {{c1::long run}}.
explanation: The 'long run' is an idiomatic expression referring to a long period of time in the future, as opposed to the immediate present.
usage:
Pattern: in the long run / long-term
  • This software is expensive, but it will save us money in the long run.
  • Our decisions must benefit the project in the long run.
spanish: Nuestra meta es llegar a un acuerdo de beneficio mutuo que beneficie a ambas partes a largo plazo.

text: We cannot accept these terms; they do not align with our risk management {{c1::guidelines}}.
explanation: 'Guidelines' are official rules, principles, or instructions that dictate how a task or business policy must be conducted.
usage:
Pattern: guidelines / company policy
  • Please review the branding guidelines before making the logo.
  • Our risk management guidelines prohibit this setup.
spanish: No podemos aceptar estas condiciones; no se alinean con nuestras directrices/pautas de gestión de riesgos.

text: Let's discuss the pricing structure: is there any room for a discount on {{c1::bulk orders}}?
explanation: A 'bulk order' (or bulk purchase) is an order for a very large quantity of a product, which typically qualifies for a discount.
usage:
Pattern: bulk order / bulk discount
  • We save 20% on paper supplies by placing a bulk order.
  • The supplier offers a discount for bulk orders.
spanish: Hablemos de la estructura de precios: ¿hay algún margen para un descuento en pedidos al por mayor?

text: We value our partnership and want to find a solution that doesn't {{c1::compromise quality}}.
explanation: To 'compromise quality' means to accept a lower standard or degrade the quality of a product or service, often to save money or time.
usage:
Pattern: compromise quality / lower standards
  • We want to reduce costs, but we will never compromise quality.
  • Cutting budget now might compromise the quality of the release.
spanish: Valoramos nuestra asociación y queremos encontrar una solución que no comprometa la calidad.

text: Could we extend the contract duration in exchange for a lower annual service {{c1::fee}}?
explanation: A 'service fee' is a charge made by a business for performing work or services for a client (distinct from product cost).
usage:
Pattern: service fee / annual fee
  • The contract includes an annual maintenance service fee.
  • The bank charges a service fee for international transfers.
spanish: ¿Podríamos extender la duración del contrato a cambio de una tarifa/cuota de servicio anual más baja?

text: Before we sign the agreement, we need to consult with our legal counsel to {{c1::review the clauses}}.
explanation: To 'review the clauses' means to read and evaluate the specific sections, paragraphs, or provisions of a legal contract to ensure they are acceptable.
usage:
Pattern: review the clauses / contract clauses
  • Our legal team needs to review the liability clauses.
  • We requested changes to the termination clauses.
spanish: Antes de firmar el acuerdo, necesitamos consultar con nuestro asesor legal para revisar las cláusulas.

text: We have reached an agreement on the scope, so let's move forward and {{c1::finalize the contract}}.
explanation: To 'finalize the contract' means to complete the last details of a legal agreement and prepare it for signing by both parties.
usage:
Pattern: finalize the contract / sign contract
  • We are meeting tomorrow to finalize the contract.
  • Once we finalize the contract, we can begin production.
spanish: Hemos llegado a un acuerdo sobre el alcance, así que sigamos adelante y finalicemos el contrato.
